long_term_goal: 100% of invited members complete onboarding within 48 hours and reach the squad list without needing help from the secretary.
sprint_questions: What is the minimum information we need to collect from a new member?
How do we make the onboarding flow feel like a welcome, not a form?
What does the secretary see when a member completes onboarding?
key_decision: Proceed with invite-link onboarding model. Three-step flow: name → email → position. User/Member entity separation adopted as a hard architectural constraint.
elias_dissent: Challenged the invite-link model on adoption grounds: 'An invite-link model assumes the secretary is proactive enough to send invites. In practice, they won't.' Overruled on GDPR grounds. Review trigger: if adoption < 60% of invited members completing registration.
next_action: Build onboarding flow against creative-brief.md. Run Spike 001 (push notifications) before Sprint 002 begins.
open_questions: Can we deliver reliable push notifications via a PWA without a native app?
What happens to an invite link that is never used?
Does the secretary need invite link analytics?
